title: galium commune var. oxyphyllum
intro: Galium commune var. oxyphyllum, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a perennial plant species with creeping stems and leaves that resemble clover. The medicinal properties of Wood Sorrel include its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Wood Sorrel can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in salad dressings and as a natural food preservative.
